title: Justin Bieber - Live at Madison Square Garden
subTitle: The film follows the pop star Justin Bieber during 10 days counting down to his performance at Madison Square Garden.

title: Robert Plant - Live in London 2014
subTitle: ITunes Festival, is an annual music festival that takes place over a month in London. Sponsored by Apple Inc., it had its first edition in 2007.

title: Blondie -- Live in London 2014
subTitle: Filmed at Roundhouse, London, giving all fans an experience; Blondie performs their hits including "Heart of Glass," "Euphoria," "Mile High" and many more.

title: Beck -- Live in London 2014
subTitle: Filmed at Roundhouse, London, this concert film will give all fans an experience; Beck performs his hits including "Loser," "Wave," "Debra" and many more.

title: Imagine Dragons - Live At The Moody Theater
subTitle: Filmed at The Moody Theater, Texas, this concert film will give all fans a spectacular Live experience from start to finish. Classic Imagine Dragons featuring unforgettable version of their hits: 'Demons', 'Radioactive' and many more.

title: Adele - iTunes Festival: Live in London
subTitle: Adele, a prominent English singer, performs some of her most iconic popular songs in front of a live audience in london.

title: Britney Spears - Live from London
subTitle: Filmed at Roundhouse, London, this concert film will give all fans a spectacular Live experience from start to finish. Britney Spears performs her hits including Baby One More Time, Oops! I Did It Again, Toxic and many more.

title: Maroon 5 -- Live in London 2014
subTitle: Filmed at Roundhouse, London, this Maroon 5 concert features songs such as "Moves Like Jagger," "Daylight," "This Love" and many more.

title: Kiss - Live in Nürburgring
subTitle: KISS confirms why they have been the best on the scene for nearly four decades, impressing an audience of more than 85,000 people, in a show of more than 2 hours and packed with hits, held at the Nurburgring, Germany in 2010.

title: Ziggy Marley - Live at the Filmore
subTitle: The Fillmore Auditorium is a historic concert hall in San Francisco, California. Founded by Bill Graham, it was named after being located originally on the corners of Fillmore Street and Geary Boulevard.

title: Celine Dion - That's Just The Woman In Me
subTitle: That's Just the Woman in Me is an American television special by Canadian singer Celine Dion which was aired on CBS on February 15, 2008 and was recorded at the Wiltern Theater in Los Angeles, California.

title: Coldplay - Austin City Limits
subTitle: The Austin City Limits Music Festival is an annual music festival that takes place at Zilker Park in Austin, Texas. The festival features more than 130 bands on eight stages for three days.

title: Michael Kiwanuka: Garorock Festival 2017
subTitle: Watch Michael Kiwanuka's performance at the Garorock Festival in 2017.

title: Unplugged: Summer Solstice
subTitle: Unplugged - Summer Solstice is the fourth live album, and the first in acoustic format, from the Norwegian band A-ha.

title: Oasis - Live at Wembley Arena 2008
subTitle: Filmed at Wembley Arena, London, this concert film will give all fans a spectacular Live experience from start to finish. Classic Oasis featuring unforgettable version of their hits: 'Champagne Supernova', 'Don't Look Back In Anger' and many more.

title: The Moody Blues - Days of Future Passed Live
subTitle: This film, capturing an electric evening at The Sony Centre, Toronto, where the band was joined on stage by a full orchestra for the first time ever, is the definitive performance the album Days of Future Passed.

title: The Moody Blues - Days of Future Passed Live
subTitle: This film, capturing an electric evening at The Sony Centre, Toronto, where the band was joined on stage by a full orchestra for the first time ever, is the definitive performance the album Days of Future Passed.

title: Paul McCartney - The Space Within US
subTitle: Paul McCartney presents this special performance of the US Tour, made in the United States, 11 weeks and 34 shows, playing great compositions of his solo career along with some Wings and Beatles songs.

title: Avril Lavigne - Live in Calgary
subTitle: The Canadian Avril Lavigne enchanted the public in 2007 at her show in Calgary (Canada), in which she released the album The Best Damn Thing, which sold, that same year, more than 6 million copies worldwide.

title: Rock Legends
subTitle: In the '60s, artists such as Grace Slick and Janis Joplin, were fronting A-list bands. Garage rock, all girl bands sprang up across the US in the 1960s and were playing rock 'n' roll on their own terms whilst breaking new ground.

title: Rock Legends
subTitle: New Wave and post-punk were the successors of the raw nihilism of punk. New Wave was brighter, more pop, synth-heavy and very dance-able.

title: Rock Legends
subTitle: Jethro Tull Named for no apparent reason after an 18th-century British agronomist who invented the machine drill for sowing seed, Jethro Tull has been one of the most commercially successful and eccentric progressive-rock bands.

title: Rock Legends
subTitle: The Kinks were a key part of the 1960s British Invasion, and their early hits - especially You Really Got Me and All Day and All of the Night - paved the way for the next decade's hard rock.

title: James Taylor - Austin City Limits
subTitle: The Austin City Limits Music Festival is an annual music festival that takes place at Zilker Park in Austin, Texas; the festival features more than 130 bands on eight stages for three days.

title: Foo Fighters - Austin City Limits
subTitle: The Austin City Limits Music Festival is an annual music festival that takes place at Zilker Park in Austin, Texas. The festival features more than 130 bands on eight stages for three days.

title: Green Day: Live at Fox Theater
subTitle: Green Day peforms from "21st Century Breakdown", at the Fox Theater in the band's hometown, Oakland, California.

title: Rihanna: Live at Made in America
subTitle: Rihanna performs on the first day of the Made in America Festival in 2016. Rihanna was the headliner of the festival, sampling songs from her album Anti as well as her older hits.

title: Usher - Omg Tour (Live from London)
subTitle: Treating the crowd at London's O2 Arena to his signature brand of R;B infused pop, Usher performed hits from recent albums Versus and Raymond V. Raymond, including DJ Got Us Falling in Love, There Goes My Baby, and OMG,.

title: Eric Clapton & Steve Winwood - Live from Madison Square Garden
subTitle: History rocked and rock made history during a sold-out 3-gig stand at Madison Square Garden in February 2008 when Eric Clapton and Steve Winwood performed a concert together for the first time since their days in the legendary Blind Faith in 1969.

title: Andrea Bocelli: Live in Tuscany
subTitle: Vivere: Live in Tuscany is a concert performed by classical singer Andrea Bocelli.

title: Michael Bublé: Caught in the Act
subTitle: `Caught in the Act' is the second live album released by Canadian jazz crooner Michael Bublé. It was filmed and recorded at the Wiltern Theater in Los Angeles. The filmed concert was aired on PBS as an episode of Great Performances, and the show was subsequently released on DVD along with the audio CD to create the album package, although the audio disc only featured eight of the songs from the concert.

title: Beyoncé - Sound of Change
subTitle: The Sound Of Change was a benefit concert held at Twickenham Stadium, London, England, as part of the Chime For Change initiative, founded by Beyoncé with the help of actress Salma Hayek and Gucci's creative director, Frida Giannini.

title: BLACKPINK - The Show
subTitle: The Show opened with Kill This Love, following a video featuring sights of dark and empty cities around the world.[9] Apart from live performances it also included several pre-recorded tracks.

title: One Direction - Apple Music Festival
subTitle: One Direction performs a stellar set at the 2015 edition of the Apple Music Festival. Be prepared to sing along to their catchy chart-topping hits!
